new_classes.definition_en: Water and water vapour falling under CN subheading 2201 90, including water vapour and natural water that has been filtered, sterilised, purified or softened, as well as ice and snow (HS Explanatory Note to heading 2201, paragraphs A and D), but excluding mineral water and aerated water of subheading 2201 10.

new_classes.label_en: Water treatment quality (filtered, sterilised, purified or softened)
new_classes.label_de: Wasseraufbereitungsqualität (filtriert, entkeimtes, geklärtes oder entkalktes Wasser)
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a water product indicating that it has undergone one or more treatment processes: filtration, sterilisation, purification, or softening.
Water in its gaseous state, classified under CN subheading 2201 90 00 as an 'other' water product distinct from liquid mineral water or aerated water.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria of CN 2201 90: (1) the class OtherWaterAndWaterVapour2201 is defined as a subclass of WatersInclNaturalArtificialMineralWaters2201, explicitly scoped to 'other' products not covered by 2201 10 (mineral/aerated water); (2) a WaterVapour class is introduced as a part-type to capture the water vapour criterion; (3) a WaterTreatmentQuality class is introduced to capture the filtered/sterilised/purified/softened criterion. A complement restriction distinguishes 2201 90 from sibling 2201 10. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the HS Explanatory Note paragraphs A and D (ice and snow for HS para A; ordinary natural water for HS para D) are referenced but their full scope is not individually modelled as named sub-classes — only the treatment-quality and vapour aspects are captured; (b) the 'complement' restriction is expressed as a named class exclusion rather than a fully closed disjointness axiom, which is a modelling approximation. The complement restriction uses a non-standard pattern (same class IRI for owl_class_iri and property_iri in the complement case) — the intent is to declare OtherWaterAndWaterVapour2201 as disjoint from MineralWaterOrCarbonatedWater2201.
